html:
Exclusive to:

Ancient Murals are Don't Starve Together objects, introduced in A New Reign. They are present in the Atrium Biome, appearing in bigger quantities near the Ancient Gateway. Their only purpose is to be inspected for a set of 5 different quotes for each character, giving away part of the history of the Ancient Civilization.

Upon reading a mural, the character will say their first quote. Reading the same mural will always give the same quote. In order to get the second one, the player must find another Mural, and so on. After getting the 5th inspection, the next one will cycle back to the first

Contents

  • 1 Murals
    • 1.1 1st Mural
    • 1.2 2nd Mural
    • 1.3 3rd Mural
    • 1.4 4th Mural
    • 1.5 5th Mural
  • 2 Trivia
  • 3 Gallery

Murals

1st Mural

Depicts an old and underdeveloped civilization, composed of insect (or likely arthropod) people. They live in misery, suffering from hunger and fear. This is before they discovered Nightmare Fuel.

2nd Mural

Something mysterious happens to said civilization. No character is able to decipher what is depicted, though Maxwell knows what happened.

3rd Mural

Depicts Nightmare Fuel overtaking the civilization, drowning it in darkness and giving the people "enviable" power.

4th Mural

Depicts the people shedding their skins and leaving their external body, changing their appearance completely.

5th Mural

Depicts a massively advanced civilization, with high technology and prosperity. All the problems seem to be solved. This appears to be after the Nightmare Fuel and before their eventual destruction due to the overuse of the same fuel.

Trivia

  • The Ancient Murals and other elements present in the Atrium seem to be made of a material different from Thulecite.
    • The Atrium Ancient Statues are an exception to this, having their inside made of Thulecite, though this may be a reference to the 4th Mural inspection.

Gallery

  • Both variants of the Ancient Mural.
DSTDSRoGSWHam
plainText:
Exclusive to:
Ancient Mural
1
2
Source
Atrium
Miscellaneous
"atrium_rubble"
Ancient Murals are Don't Starve Together objects, introduced in A New Reign. They are present in the Atrium Biome, appearing in bigger quantities near the Ancient Gateway. Their only purpose is to be inspected for a set of 5 different quotes for each character, giving away part of the history of the Ancient Civilization.
Upon reading a mural, the character will say their first quote. Reading the same mural will always give the same quote. In order to get the second one, the player must find another Mural, and so on. After getting the 5th inspection, the next one will cycle back to the first
Contents
1 Murals
1.1 1st Mural
1.2 2nd Mural
1.3 3rd Mural
1.4 4th Mural
1.5 5th Mural
2 Trivia
3 Gallery
Murals[edit | edit source]
1st Mural[edit | edit source]
“It depicts an old civilization. The people look hungry and scared.”
–Wilson
“It's got a picture of some gross looking people.”
–Willow
“Is picture of many sad, weak people.”
–Wolfgang
“A people struck by famine and sorrow.”
–Wendy
“IT DEPICTS INFERIOR CREATURES”
–WX-78
“An ancient mural of a non-mammalian civilization.”
–Wickerbottom
“It's an old drawing of strange creatures.”
–Woodie
“A picture of the city, before the fuel.”
–Maxwell
“Tis a pörtrait öf an ancient peöple. They live in wretchedness.”
–Wigfrid
“It's a picture of some bug people.”
–Webber
“A picture of lots of unprepared seafood. I'm hungry.”
–Warly
“Story!”
–Wormwood
“There's a picture on it of some strangely shaped people.”
–Winona
“A bunch of mortals. They look very bored.”
–Wortox
“Ooooh, picture story!”
–Wurt
“It looks like things weren't going so well for the people here.”
–Walter
“A mural of an ancient civilization, but something is wrong...”
–Wanda
Depicts an old and underdeveloped civilization, composed of insect (or likely arthropod) people. They live in misery, suffering from hunger and fear. This is before they discovered Nightmare Fuel.
2nd Mural[edit | edit source]
“This tablet is too worn to make out.”
–Wilson
“Can't tell what that was a picture of.”
–Willow
“Cannot tell what was picture of.”
–Wolfgang
“A piece of their story, lost to time.”
–Wendy
“TARGET DATA LOST”
–WX-78
“This mural panel is too eroded to decipher.”
–Wickerbottom
“Nothin' of interest left here.”
–Woodie
“We all know what happens next.”
–Maxwell
“There is nö painting here. Önly stöne.”
–Wigfrid
“This picture's all messed up.”
–Webber
“This tablet's all worn down.”
–Warly
“Can't read it”
–Wormwood
“Can't make heads or tails of this picture.”
–Winona
“There's some magic residue on the stone. Not much else.”
–Wortox
“Can't see pictures, florp.”
–Wurt
“I can't tell what's happening in this one, the picture's worn off.”
–Walter
“Whatever was here has been lost to time.”
–Wanda
Something mysterious happens to said civilization. No character is able to decipher what is depicted, though Maxwell knows what happened.
3rd Mural[edit | edit source]
“Something dark creeps over the city and its people.”
–Wilson
“I think someone spilled ink on this picture.”
–Willow
“In picture darkness covers sad, weak people.”
–Wolfgang
“They are consumed by darkness.”
–Wendy
“THE CREATURES ARE SUBMERGED IN BEAUTIFUL OIL”
–WX-78
“A dark shadow, or perhaps a substance, overtakes the civilization.”
–Wickerbottom
“Black muck is covering everything in this drawing.”
–Woodie
“They gained such an enviable power...”
–Maxwell
“A great evil sweeps acröss the land.”
–Wigfrid
“There's a lot of black goop in this picture.”
–Webber
“The world is flooded by a finely fermented black bean sauce.”
–Warly
“Black. Lots of black”
–Wormwood
“The people are drowning in axle grease.”
–Winona
“Ooo, it's about to get good!”
–Wortox
“Look like... bad things happen.”
–Wurt
“There's... a shadow? Or something... they took some artistic liberties.”
–Walter
“It shows shadows descending on the city.”
–Wanda
Depicts Nightmare Fuel overtaking the civilization, drowning it in darkness and giving the people "enviable" power.
4th Mural[edit | edit source]
“The people are shedding their skins. They look different underneath.”
–Wilson
“Oh, gross! The people're losing their skin in this one!”
–Willow
“Monsters is bursting out of them!”
–Wolfgang
“Then, rebirth.”
–Wendy
“THE CREATURES UPGRADE THEIR BODY PANELS”
–WX-78
“The citizens molt from their exoskeletons in this panel.”
–Wickerbottom
“Don't look at this one, Lucy.”
–Woodie
“I know why you led me back here.”
–Maxwell
“Many öf the peöple are cleaved in twö!”
–Wigfrid
“A picture of bug people escaping their bug outsides!”
–Webber
“The prawns being de-shelled.”
–Warly
“Oh! People changing!”
–Wormwood
“Yuck. Something grotesque is happening in this picture.”
–Winona
“What a hoot! The mortals are slithering out their skins!”
–Wortox
“This getting scary...”
–Wurt
“Woah, looks like they all started changing.”
–Walter
“The people weren't quick enough to escape. They paid a price...”
–Wanda
Depicts the people shedding their skins and leaving their external body, changing their appearance completely.
5th Mural[edit | edit source]
“It shows a massive, technologically advanced city.”
–Wilson
“It's just a picture of a city.”
–Willow
“Is picture of very pretty place.”
–Wolfgang
“Finally, a city, full of life.”
–Wendy
“IT SHOWS A CITY MADE OF SHINING METAL”
–WX-78
“The city appears exponentially more prosperous.”
–Wickerbottom
“Looks like a bustling city.”
–Woodie
“But it won't work.”
–Maxwell
“A glittering city, blessed by the göds.”
–Wigfrid
“A picture of a city. It looks like a nice place to live.”
–Webber
“The prawns have ascended into deliciousness.”
–Warly
“Pretty picture!”
–Wormwood
“A picture of a beautiful, well engineered city.”
–Winona
“They probably shouldn't have used that much magic.”
–Wortox
“They had village here.”
–Wurt
“That must be what the city looked like a long time ago.”
–Walter
“They had the right idea for a while, but they must have made a mistake.”
–Wanda
Depicts a massively advanced civilization, with high technology and prosperity. All the problems seem to be solved. This appears to be after the Nightmare Fuel and before their eventual destruction due to the overuse of the same fuel.
Trivia[edit | edit source]
The Ancient Murals and other elements present in the Atrium seem to be made of a material different from Thulecite.
The Atrium Ancient Statues are an exception to this, having their inside made of Thulecite, though this may be a reference to the 4th Mural inspection.
Gallery[edit | edit source]
Both variants of the Ancient Mural.
DSTDSRoGSWHam

summaryViHtml:
Tóm tắt

Những bức tranh tường cổ là vật thể của Don't Starve Together, được giới thiệu trong A New Reign. Chúng hiện diện trong Atrium Biome, xuất hiện với số lượng lớn hơn ở gần Ancient Gateway. Mục đích duy nhất của họ là kiểm tra bộ 5 câu trích dẫn khác nhau cho mỗi nhân vật, tiết lộ một phần lịch sử của Nền văn minh cổ đại.
title: Ancient Mural

|biome = [[Atrium]]
|spawnCode = "atrium_rubble"}}
'''Ancient Murals''' are ''[[Don't Starve Together]]'' objects, introduced in ''[[A New Reign]]''. They are present in the [[Atrium]] Biome, appearing in bigger quantities near the [[Ancient Gateway]]. Their only purpose is to be inspected for a set of 5 different quotes for each character, giving away part of the history of the [[Ancient Civilization]].

Upon reading a mural, the character will say their first quote. Reading the same mural will always give the same quote. In order to get the second one, the player must find another Mural, and so on. After getting the 5th inspection, the next one will cycle back to the first
